task_completion.description: Visit the target websites (CNN, Weather.com, Wikipedia)
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a web browser open and navigating to cnn.com, weather.com, and/or wikipedia.org (visible in the address bar or active tabs).

task_completion.description: Inspect the cookies deposited by the websites
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the browser's Developer Tools (e.g., Application tab -> Cookies) or browser settings open, displaying the cookies stored for the visited sites.
A text editor or word processor open, showing a file saved as or being edited at '/home/ga/Desktop/cookie_forensic_report.txt'.

task_completion.description: Include cookie counts for each site in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the report explicitly lists the number of cookies found for CNN, Weather.com, and Wikipedia.

task_completion.description: Identify specific third-party tracking domains in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the report names specific third-party tracking domains found during the inspection (e.g., DoubleClick, Criteo, Scorecardresearch, Google Analytics).

task_completion.description: Provide a comparative analysis of tracking footprints
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the report includes a comparison or conclusion discussing which of the three sites has the heaviest tracking footprint or most cookies/trackers.

integrity.description: The agent used a real web browser to visit the sites and inspect cookies
integrity.visual_evidence: A GUI web browser (like Chrome, Firefox, or Chromium) is visible in the screenshots, rather than the agent just writing a script or hallucinating the data.

integrity.description: The agent actually inspected the cookies rather than guessing
integrity.visual_evidence: Developer tools or cookie management interfaces are visible showing actual cookie data for the target domains.

integrity.description: The report content is based on actual findings, not just copy-pasted examples
integrity.visual_evidence: The report contains specific numbers and domain names that match what would be found in a real browser session, rather than just repeating the prompt's examples verbatim without context.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target domains are cnn.com, weather.com, and wikipedia.org. The report must be saved to /home/ga/Desktop/cookie_forensic_report.txt. Known legitimate tracking domains that might be found include doubleclick, google-analytics, googletagmanager, facebook, scorecardresearch, amazon-adsystem, adsrvr, criteo, rubiconproject, pubmatic, outbrain, and taboola.
